item.title: Bitcoin Campaign to Force SNB Holdings Has Failed, Reuters Says
item.description: Swiss crypto campaigners have abandoned a bid to require the central bank to hold Bitcoin after failing to collect enough signatures to trigger a plebiscite, Reuters reported.

item.title: Orange Juice Jumps Most in a Week on Shrinking Brazil Crop
item.description: Orange juice futures surged in New York on an outlook for a weaker harvest of the fruit in Brazil, the world’s top exporter of the juice.

item.title: Siemens Scores Win on EU Push to Streamline Industrial AI Rules
item.description: Siemens AG and other European tech companies have made headway on reshaping regulation for artificial intelligence in the European Union they say is putting them behind rivals in the US and China.

item.title: Hormuz Transits Halted Since Tuesday as US, Iran Clash
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to commercial shipping after the US and Iran clashed overnight near the waterway, attacking each other’s assets in the area.

item.title: BOE Governor Says UK Needs Closer EU Ties in a Superpower Age
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey called for a rebuilding of trade relations with Europe, warning that Britain needs allies in a world dominated by competing superpowers.

item.title: Pentagon Releases Declassified UFO Files on Government Website
item.description: The Pentagon announced that it has begun releasing government files on UFOs after President Donald Trump directed agencies to identify and declassify them.

item.title: Itau Asset’s Serra Sees Bulk of Brazil Rate Cuts After Elections
item.description: The bulk of interest-rate cuts in Brazil is likely to come only after October elections, according to former Central Bank director Bruno Serra.

item.title: Hassett, Asked on Gundlach Debt Idea, Says Not in Million Years
item.description: White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett suggested that investor Jeffrey Gundlach’s scenario for a type of restructuring of US Treasury securities would amount to a default, and rejected any such move.

item.title: Giant Data Center Project in Virginia Upended by Clerical Error
item.description: Developers bankrolled by two of the world’s biggest asset managers set their sights on building a massive data-center hub in Northern Virginia. A fight over newspaper advertising threw their plans into disarray.

item.title: Hormuz Woes Seen Lingering Into Second Half, Goldman Poll Shows
item.description: Wall Street is growing more convinced that shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will remain impaired into the second half of the year, highlighting expectations for a longer-lasting supply shock.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Falls to Record Low on Inflation Angst
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell in recent weeks to a fresh record low on concerns about the impact of inflation on personal finances and buying conditions.

item.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways on the US Jobs Report for April
item.description: Here are the key takeaways from the US employment report for April, released Friday:

item.title: Enhanced Games Falls 14% After SPAC Merger at $1.2 Billion Value
item.description: Enhanced Group Inc. shares sank 14% after going public via a merger with blank-check company A Paradise Acquisition Corp. that gave it an enterprise value of $1.2 billion.

item.title: Online Mob Fuels 6,000% Stock Rally in Obscure SpaceX Rival
item.description: Championed by a guru known simply as the Kook, the satellite company AST has become one of the world's most expensive stocks.

item.title: Aramco, Adnoc Sneak Oil Through Hormuz as Iran Menaces Strait
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s Aramco Trading Co. and the UAE’s state oil company Adnoc are among firms that have moved crude cargoes through the Strait of Hormuz since Iran effectively closed the waterway, according to people familiar with the situation.

item.title: Ukraine Says It Hit Two Major Oil Refineries in Russia Overnight
item.description: Ukraine said it struck two major fuel-producing facilities and an oil pumping station in Russia, adding more pressure on the nation’s refinery runs that hit multi-year lows in April.

item.title: Turkey Wheat Output Seen Rebounding After Drought-Hit 2025
item.description: Turkey’s wheat harvest is poised for a sharp recovery from last year’s crop-ravaging droughts, potentially curbing the outlook for imports from one of the world’s largest wheat buyers.

item.title: Russia’s Budget Gap Widens to Record Despite Oil Revenue Boost
item.description: Russia’s budget gap widened to a record despite a jump in oil revenue amid heightened tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: US Jobs Up 115,000, First Back-to-Back Gain in Nearly a Year
item.description: US employers added to payrolls for a second month in April, marking the first back-to-back advance in nearly a year, and the unemployment rate held steady.

item.title: Canada Employment Fell by 17,700, Jobless Rate Climbs to 6.9%
item.description: The Canadian economy shed 17,700 jobs in April while more people looked for work, pushing the unemployment rate up to 6.9%.

item.title: Argentina Soyoil Trades at Decade Low to US on Harvest, Biofuels
item.description: Soybean oil from Argentina, the world’s biggest exporter, is trading at the cheapest in at least a decade relative to rival supplies out of the US, as the harvest on the Pampas crop belt hits full stride and after Washington increased biofuel blending mandates.

item.title: Chile Hit by Biggest Consumer Price Jump Since 2022 on Fuel Hike
item.description: Chile’s consumer prices in April posted the biggest monthly jump since 2022, reflecting the brunt of of President José Antonio Kast’s decision to let fuel costs surge after the onset of the Middle East war.

item.title: Elliott Loses Fight Over $8.7 Billion Buyout of China’s 58.com
item.description: Elliott Investment Management, T. Rowe Price Group Inc. and other money managers failed to persuade a Cayman Islands judge to award more than the deal price in the $8.7 billion take-private of China’s largest online classified-ad marketplace six years ago.

item.title: Economists Cast Doubt on Milei’s Green Shoots in Weary Argentina
item.description: President Javier Milei and leading experts are at odds over whether Argentina’s economy has recovered from a recent wobble, highlighting the challenges involved in gauging whether the reformer’s shock therapy policies are working as intended.

item.title: Record French Solar Power Output Pushes Prices Below Zero
item.description: French solar output surged to a record on Friday, exceeding grid operator forecasts and pushing power prices below zero.

item.title: Fannie, Freddie’s Latest Bull Says Traders Underpricing IPO Odds
item.description: Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac investors are underestimating the chances of a public market re-entry from the mortgage giants after a lull in chatter around the names, according to Mizuho’s Dan Dolev.

item.title: Southeast Asia Boosts Maritime Ties to Avoid Hormuz-Like Crisis
item.description: Southeast Asian nations agreed to boost cooperation and create a center for maritime issues, which the Philippines said could help avert a situation in the South China Sea similar to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Can Companies Push Price This Time?
item.description: Maybe it’s tougher.

item.title: Trump OKs US Oil Pipe, Undercutting Canadian Plan Backed by Carney
item.description: Ottawa wants to weaken its dependence on the US, but Washington has other ideas.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 8, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as traders await Iran’s response to a US-proposed deal to end the war.
